prompt: What came out of yesterday's OAC?

failures: expected at least one of [getMeetingsByDate, searchMeetingsByTopic, getActionItemsAndInsights] to fire — none did
score.finalText:
Yesterday’s OAC output, if you mean the **Vermillion Rise Warehouse** one, was mostly **contract cleanup before execution** — not field progress.

### What came out of it
- **They aligned key A141 contract terms.**
  - Progress was made on **dispute resolution**
  - **Subcontractor approval language**
  - **Indemnification**
  - **Payment timelines**

- **They agreed to tighten the payment dispute process.**
  - Partial payments during disputes were accepted.
  - Notice period for withholding certification was pushed to **15 days**
  - Resolution period set at **45 days**

- **The team wants the dispute clock to start earlier.**
  - Brandon pushed for the clock to start **upon certification of the payment issue**, not after more back-and-forth. That matters because it cuts off drift and keeps disputes from lingering.

- **Vendor/subcontractor risk controls were emphasized.**
  - Early **credit checks and vendor vetting** were called out as important to avoid financial and schedule problems later.

### Action items that came out of it
- Review and refine language around:
  - **change orders**
  - **mechanics’ liens**
  - **dispute resolution**
- Draft revisions for:
  - **subcontractor approval notification timing and thresholds**
- Update language to:
  - **exclude permit costs from the contract sum** and clarify reimbursement
- Revise punch list language to require:
  - work to **“commence within”** the required timeframe
  - diligence language for **lead-time-driven items**
- Finalize payment withholding language around:
  - **15-day notice**
  - **45-day resolution**

### My read
This was a **risk-control meeting**, not a production meeting. The good news is they’re closing contract ambiguity now instead of fighting about it later. The issue is that **none of this matters unless Logan turns revisions quickly and the team closes the remaining redlines before closing/execution**.

### What to do next
Get **Logan’s revised draft** back in circulation immediately and make sure the next review focuses on only the unresolved items:
1. dispute language  
2. sub approval thresholds  
3. permit reimbursement  
4. punch list timing  
5. payment withholding mechanics  

If you want, I can also pull the **full meeting details with speaker-level notes** and give you a tighter “who said what / what still isn’t resolved” readout.

data.message: Reading conversation memory and project context

output.title: Meeting summary
output.subtitle: Structured readout from meeting records, extracted insights, and transcript summaries
topInsights: 19 risk signals found across the matched meetings.
19 decision signals found across the matched meetings.
recommendedNextActions: Open the meeting cards with risk or action badges first.
Convert verified ownership items into Tasks page records.
Use the linked meeting records as the evidence trail before sending an owner update.
output.emptyState: No meeting rows matched this request.

meetings.title: Cost Coding and Approval CC Transactions & Time Sheets
meetings.summary:
📈 New Team Expansion: Five new hires added to handle projects valued at nearly $60 million, boosting current team from 4 to over 20.
💳 Improving Cost Coding: Instructional videos for new staff and increased PM oversight aimed at reducing billing delays and ...
criticalRisks: Manpower scheduling critical to balance current and upcoming jobs.

- **Current credit card transaction process causes billing delays and reporting roadblocks** as receipts often lack proper job codes or cost categories, making monthly reporting impossible until resolved (01:50) - Jesse plans to create instructional videos for new staff on correct coding procedures to reduce errors - Ashley will take responsibility for reviewing and verifying transactions to speed up approvals - The team acknowledged this task is critical to close out jobs and complete monthly financial reports - **PMs to increase involvement in weekly/monthly transaction reviews** to better control spending and coding accuracy, potentially during Friday weekly report meetings (18:30) - Kebba suggested a weekly review to catch common mistakes and improve consistency - Jesse emphasized the need for PMs to work closely with supers on coding before final approval to prevent errors and save time - **System visibility and permissions for transaction management explained** with Kebba showing dashboard views and filtering options to monitor all employee expenses (19:50) - Jesse demonstrated the ability to identify incorrect coding, missing receipts, and timing issues on charges - Kebba confirmed PMs can edit and correct cost coding to reduce administrative burden on Jesse - The goal is to empower field leadership to catch mistakes early, reducing the volume of required rework - **Awareness raised on spending habits and vendor costs** with Gregory investigating overcharges on car rentals to ensure compliance with quoted rates (17:30) - Jesse reminded the team to seek bargains and maintain cost-consciousness despite having spending freedoms - Monitoring and controlling discretionary costs is necessary to protect project budgets ## **Project Pipeline and Workload Management** The team is managing a robust pipeline of sprinkler projects and other builds with detailed scheduling and manpower alignment.

- **Copying senior PMs Doug and Bob on key emails is mandated to leverage their experience and maintain alignment** (00:55) - Jesse urged everyone to keep upper management informed on decisions and issues to benefit from their knowledge - This helps avoid miscommunication and keeps leadership in the loop for critical matters - **Monthly billing summaries to be shared with Andrew and Bryce to improve project financial oversight** (02:10) - Doug may review these summaries monthly with Andrew and Bryce to ensure transparency on project build values - This is intended to keep all stakeholders aware of revenue progress and job status - **Jesse plans to schedule next week’s meetings with a split focus between Doug’s and Bob’s teams** to streamline coordination across offices (00:55) - This will help reduce confusion for those working across both groups - Emphasizes clearer lines of communication and responsibility - **Team members encouraged to report manpower schedules and project statuses regularly** to help recruiting and PMs anticipate staffing needs (15:30) - Jesse emphasized using Teams messages or calls to update Jasmine’s spreadsheet promptly - This continuous communication loop supports proactive hiring and resource allocation ## **Productivity Culture and Workload Insights** The team is maintaining strong productivity with high workloads balanced by strategic hiring and process improvements.
decisions: Additionally, inclusion of senior PMs in key communications aims to enhance oversight and decision-making, while maintaining a positive office culture amidst growth.

## Shorthand Bullet 📧 **Communication Protocols** (01:00 - 01:30) Emphasis on copying senior PMs and upper management on project correspondence to keep them informed and leverage their knowledge for decision-making.

- **Adding five new team members to cover high-value projects** including Tony (PM starting Monday), Demetrius (assistant PM), Patrick Antonella (project engineer), and two interns, Victor and Hunter, who will assist Nick Bremmy and estimating respectively (08:30) - Tony will focus on the Exo and Vermillion projects, both valued near **$60 million** combined - Patrick has prior experience at FA Wilhelm, expected to bring valuable expertise, especially on large projects like XL Wilmer - Intern Victor supports Illinois projects, while Hunter helps in estimating to alleviate workload - Jesse emphasized the importance of quickly integrating these hires to maintain momentum on multiple concurrent projects - **Recruiting and manpower scheduling improvements underway** with Jasmine managing a dynamic spreadsheet to track project start and stop dates and manpower needs (14:50) - Kebba confirmed current coverage for ongoing projects including Utah and Florida, with potential hiring if new projects arise - Jesse stressed keeping updated manpower plans to avoid last-minute scrambling and overhiring risks - This system aims to balance staffing against workload to avoid firing or underutilization - **Experienced hires bring diverse backgrounds to strengthen team** with Tony’s GC business contacts expected to open new business opportunities in Indianapolis (07:30) - Jesse highlighted Tony’s semi-retired status but strong network as a strategic asset to convert leads into work - Patrick’s transition from teaching to construction management adds valuable project oversight skills for complex builds - **Team morale and office culture remain positive despite growth** with light-hearted banter and recognition of busy schedules, reflecting good camaraderie as team size grows from **4 to over 20** in recent years (05:50) - Jesse noted the pace and scale increase from **$10 million to $28 million** in revenue alongside team expansion - Andrew and Candon exchanged friendly comments, reinforcing a supportive work environment ## **Financial Processes and Cost Control** Improving credit card transaction oversight and cost coding accuracy is a priority to avoid billing delays and budget overruns.
actionItems: Copy senior PMs and upper management on all project correspondence to keep them informed and leverage their expertise for decision-making (01:00)
Collaborate with superintendents to properly code and approve credit card transactions
create training videos for new staff on this process (02:40)
Send updated list of jobs and billing amounts to Andrew and Bryce
coordinate monthly billing reviews with Doug (03:44)

meetings.title: A141 Discussion
meetings.summary:
✅ Contract Terms Alignment: Progress on dispute resolution, subcontractor approvals, indemnification, and payment timelines was achieved.
⚖️ Dispute Resolution Preference: Litigation favored for flexibility; will discuss further to finalize language.
💼 Subco...
criticalRisks: - Brandon requested the dispute clock start promptly upon certification of payment issues, not delayed, to avoid prolonged back-and-forth.
## **Subcontractor and Supplier Oversight** The parties worked toward a clear framework for managing subcontractors and suppliers to control risk and ensure financial soundness.
- Mark stressed the importance of early credit checks and vendor vetting to prevent financial risk and delays.
decisions: For payments, partial payments during disputes were agreed upon, with timelines for notification and resolution set at 15 days and 45 days respectively.
Next steps involve Logan integrating the agreed changes for final review before contract execution following upcoming closing.
💰 **Payment Withholding and Dispute Resolution** (34:00 - 51:30) 15-day period agreed for decision-making on withholding payment certifications, reduced from 30 days.
actionItems: Review detailed contract provisions on change orders, mechanics' liens, and dispute resolution to refine language (30:07)
Draft revisions to subcontractor approval notification process including timing and thresholds and circulate for review (11:00)
Adjust permit cost reimbursement language to clearly exclude permit costs from contract sum and address reimbursement (25:40)
Modify punch list completion timing language to include “commence within” and diligence clauses addressing lead times (52:20)
Refine notification and dispute resolution timelines for payment withholding to 15-day notice and 45-day resolution periods (43:30)

meetings.title: Alleato QuickBooks transfer discussion
meetings.summary:
📈 Change in Accounting Software: Team shifted focus to QuickBooks, abandoning Acumatica due to complexity and poor support.
🔄 Simplified Retainage Handling: QuickBooks uses negative invoice line items for retainage, easing current pain points with Acumatica...
criticalRisks: - The test account approach is low risk and allows discovery of potential blockers without impacting live operations.
- This phased approach mitigates risk and allows adjustment based on pilot results.
[02:12] **Brandon Clymer**: Now as far as the reporting functions in some of the, some of the Ars and reports that QuickBooks can do, I concern me a little bit.
decisions: ## Short Summary The meeting centered on the decision to move from using Acumatica to QuickBooks as their primary accounting software.

## Notes ## **Accounting Software Strategy** The team decided to move away from pursuing a deal with **Acumatica** due to limited improvement opportunities and customer support issues, focusing instead on evaluating **QuickBooks** as a simpler alternative (00:21).
- Issues with retainage processing in Acumatica have caused ongoing headaches, confirmed by emails from Jesse.
actionItems: Coordinate with the accounting team to develop a detailed migration plan and data handling approach for transitioning from Acumatica to QuickBooks (11:44)
Identify and select specific test jobs to pilot the QuickBooks integration using a separate test QuickBooks account (10:36)
Follow up with the Job Planner team to review the QuickBooks test setup and resolve any integration issues identified during the trial phase (17:19)
Support the setup of a separate QuickBooks test account and assist with integrating selected test projects to validate the migration approach (13:25)
Provide guidance on differences in retainage handling and purchase order processing between Acumatica and QuickBooks to facilitate smooth migration (07:00)

meetings.title: Interview Accountant position (Lauren Rohlfs)
meetings.summary:
📊 Candidate Experience: Lauren has 15-16 years in accounting, including tax prep and bookkeeping for multiple accounts.
💻 Software Proficiency: She is skilled in QuickBooks and Paylocity, with basic Excel and Outlook knowledge.
🤝 Cultural Fit: Lauren is co...
criticalRisks: Internal discussion noted slight concern about actual length of Lauren's experience but overall positive impression.
No anticipated short-term retirement risk due to candidate's relative youth.

- **Remote work readiness and independence** were highlighted as critical, with Lauren comfortable working alone and managing priorities without direct supervision (06:36) - She demonstrated adaptability by handling interruptions and shifting tasks efficiently, which is essential in a dynamic remote environment.
decisions: ## Notes ## **Candidate Evaluation and Hiring Decision** The meeting focused on assessing **Lauren Rohlfs’ bookkeeping and accounting experience** and deciding on her potential hiring for a remote accounting role.

- **Lauren’s accounting background and readiness for full-time work** were confirmed, highlighting approximately **15-16 years of experience** including tax preparation and bookkeeping for multiple accounts (03:17) - She worked in various roles, including CPA firms and electrical contracting bookkeeping, handling up to **12 accounts** simultaneously with bank reconciliations and credit card transactions.

- **Salary and employment terms** were agreed upon, with Lauren accepting a **1099 independent contractor position** and a **pay range between $15 and $20 per hour**, ideally around **$19 to $20** (11:15) - The team plans to start her near the end of the current week, with the offer contingent on internal approval from CEO Brandon and potential reference checks.
actionItems: Provide feedback and final decision on Lauren Rohlfs's candidacy to HR and CEO after reviewing additional candidate results (18:50)
Manage communication with the temporary staffing service regarding termination of the current temp worker, Jose (19:09)
Inform CEO Brandon about Lauren Rohlfs's interview and positive impression
seek CEO's input on hiring decision (16:02)
Prepare and send an offer letter to Lauren Rohlfs with proposed hourly pay between $19 and $20 upon CEO's approval (16:02)

meetings.title: Uniqlo & GPC Huddle
meetings.summary:
🔧 Racking Installation Progress: 90% pipe built at Siva site, awaiting permit approval next weekend for installation.
💨 Air Pressure Testing: Ongoing at Uniqlo; compressor onsite while waiting for equipment delivery to proceed.
🏗️ Vendor Accounts Installat...
criticalRisks: Air pressure testing is ongoing at Uniqlo, with equipment delivery anticipated, while the GPC site is addressing pipe leaks and expediting procurement for critical parts.
System A partially completed with gap due to flu pipe delay.
## Notes ## **Project Progress and Site Updates** The teams are steadily advancing on pipe rack installations and system air testing despite some delays caused by missing components.
decisions: 🚧 Pipe Replacement Decision: Visible corrosion necessitates replacement for aesthetic and quality assurance, with documentation for validation.
- **Pipe Replacement Decision:** Visible corrosion necessitates replacement for aesthetic and quality assurance, with documentation for validation.

- **90% of pipe built for next aisle at Siva site with readiness to install upon permit approval** (13:11) - Racking and pipe assembly are nearly complete, awaiting work permit expected by next weekend - TJ Graves noted the ability to shift crews to GPC site to maintain pace with racking team - Weekend work with 3-4 personnel is planned to keep schedule on track - This flexibility helps mitigate potential permit delays and resource constraints - **Air pressure testing ongoing at Uniqlo, pending equipment delivery from Equipment Share** (06:50) - Compressor was onsite for systems A and B and system 3 air testing - Parker Hollingsworth supervising punch list and pipe painting with one worker assigned to A line guards - Equipment Share invoicing and account setup discussed to streamline billing and payments - Prompt setup of vendor accounts and W9 collection prioritized for smooth invoicing - **GPC site making steady progress with branch pipe installation and air leak fixes on couplings** (06:17) - No holes detected yet; focus on replacing leaking couplings to maintain system integrity - Procurement for missing six-inch couplings and tees expedited through One Industrial to meet next-day delivery needs - Decision to bypass usual supplier Akifier due to lead time constraints illustrates agile supply chain management - **Racking drawings for Exalt site partially available; structural and reaction data pending full sets** (23:40) - Symbotic call updates revealed ongoing revisions incorporating electric overlays and data drops - Use of Revit files creates compatibility challenges for some team members, requiring technical support - Maintaining clarity on documentation status is critical for coordination and scheduling ## **Material Handling and Quality Assurance** Material inspection and pipe condition assessments emphasize thorough documentation and strategic replacement to satisfy stakeholders.
actionItems: Add Equipment Share to the project system and obtain their W9 form to set up billing and invoicing processes for air compressor rental costs (07:42)
Arrange procurement account setup with Wind Industrial to enable faster ordering and procurement of materials (08:48)
Initiate creation of a racking schedule/tracker file for McLean and new projects to document weekly progress and maintain organization (28:45)
Order six-inch pipe couplings, cones, and mechanical tees and arrange for next-day pickup from One Industrial due to urgent timing needs (05:22)
Monitor GPC site for any air leaks during branch line installation and report immediately to coordinate repairs if leaks are detected (06:36)

meetings.summary:

decisions: ## Shorthand Bullet 👋 **Meeting Start and Attendance** (00:00 - 01:00) Team attendance confirmed.
Mechanical yard location confirmed with screening from US 42.

- **Concerns about 15-17% slope on site impacting maintenance and lawn care** with mobile slope classification confirmed for some areas (29:30) - Steep slopes may challenge mowing and upkeep with tractors or implements.
actionItems: Provide solar panel roofing system design information to Nick Vrettos, Jerome Daksiewicz, and Jim Burke to understand electrical sizing impact and jurisdictional coordination (58:30)
Confirm with Westfield General Managers about rooftop unit and hot water heater performance to identify any operational issues (57:40)
Push for final plan clarifications and layout confirmations with the owner to meet the May 22 deadline for furniture and kitchen equipment layouts (01:10:40)
Coordinate with Douglas Franklin to communicate owner updates promptly to architecture and engineering teams to avoid delays (01:20:35)
Develop and share refined site design including building rotation and entry point adjustments addressing grading and detention pond concerns (24:30)

meetings.summary: The team discussed strict hiring standards, deciding to disqualify candidates who missed interviews without notice, emphasizing reliability. They reviewed the restructuring of the payroll and accounting setup due to inefficiencies revealed in audits and poor ...

meetings.title: Estimating Intro (making Calls)
meetings.summary: The team outlined an efficient bid management process utilizing Building Connected to streamline subcontractor communication and engagement. Key initiatives included a structured bid package setup for Project 440, ensuring organized invitations and file shari...

- **Real-time Tracking of Subcontractor Engagement** helps monitor who views bids and actively participates, enhancing communication and follow-up efficiency.
- **Multi-channel Follow-up Strategy** includes calls and emails to ensure subcontractors confirm receipt of bids and maintain engagement.

meetings.title: Onboarding review and Office Introduction
meetings.summary: The onboarding session was designed to prepare new interns for their roles by ensuring they completed crucial tasks such as logging into Teams, Outlook, Bluebeam, and Job Planner. Victor and Hunter confirmed access, and Jazmin verified that all interns were s...
criticalRisks: - **Ongoing Support Guaranteed:** Jazmin and Ashley maintained communication channels for rapid issue resolution and guidance throughout onboarding.
decisions: Victor and Hunter confirmed access, and Jazmin verified that all interns were set for immediate work use.

meetings.title: Weekly Schedule Review Goodwill Allisonville Road Teams Meeting
meetings.summary: The meeting outlined the current construction progress focusing on site work, including demolition, steel framing, and concrete preparations. Coordination between team members is emphasized, with plumbing pours scheduled after framing. There are ongoing delay...
actionItems: There are ongoing delays in door frame orders due to submittal issues, risking project timelines as typical lead times are six to eight weeks.
Material procurement is mixed, with HVAC components ordered efficiently, but door frames are pending due to plywood pricing confirmation.
- **Door Frame Delays:** Door frame orders on hold due to submittal delays; typical lead time is six to eight weeks.

input.message: What came out of yesterday's OAC?

output.rationale: The user is asking for outcomes from a recent OAC meeting, which is a request for current project status and meeting takeaways. However, no project is selected, so the assistant should first clarify which project or OAC meeting they mean before retrieving or summarizing results.

output.reason: Streaming model tools enabled on the configured AI SDK provider. Vercel AI Gateway is preferred when AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is configured; direct OpenAI requires AI_PROVIDER_PATH=openai or no gateway key. Disable by setting AI_ASSISTANT_DISABLE_STREAMING_MODEL_TOOLS=true.

💨 Air Pressure Testing: Ongoing at Uniqlo; compressor onsite while waiting for equipment delivery to proceed.
🏗️ Vendor Accounts Installat...
decisions: 🚧 Pipe Replacement Decision: Visible corrosion necessitates replacement for aesthetic and quality assurance, with documentation for validation.
- **Pipe Replacement Decision:** Visible corrosion necessitates replacement for aesthetic and quality assurance, with documentation for validation.

- **90% of pipe built for next aisle at Siva site with readiness to install upon permit approval** (13:11) - Racking and pipe assembly are nearly complete, awaiting work permit expected by next weekend - TJ Graves noted the ability to shift crews to GPC site to maintain pace with racking team - Weekend work with 3-4 personnel is planned to keep schedule on track - This flexibility helps mitigate potential permit delays and resource constraints - **Air pressure testing ongoing at Uniqlo, pending equipment delivery from Equipment Share** (06:50) - Compressor was onsite for systems A and B and system 3 air testing - Parker Hollingsworth supervising punch list and pipe painting with one worker assigned to A line guards - Equipment Share invoicing and account setup discussed to streamline billing and payments - Prompt setup of vendor accounts and W9 collection prioritized for smooth invoicing - **GPC site making steady progress with branch pipe installation and air leak fixes on couplings** (06:17) - No holes detected yet; focus on replacing leaking couplings to maintain system integrity - Procurement for missing six-inch couplings and tees expedited through One Industrial to meet next-day delivery needs - Decision to bypass usual supplier Akifier due to lead time constraints illustrates agile supply chain management - **Racking drawings for Exalt site partially available; structural and reaction data pending full sets** (23:40) - Symbotic call updates revealed ongoing revisions incorporating electric overlays and data drops - Use of Revit files creates compatibility challenges for some team members, requiring technical support - Maintaining clarity on documentation status is critical for coordination and scheduling ## **Material Handling and Quality Assurance** Material inspection and pipe condition assessments emphasize thorough documentation and strategic replacement to satisfy stakeholders.
actionItems: Add Equipment Share to the project system and obtain their W9 form to set up billing and invoicing processes for air compressor rental costs (07:42)
Arrange procurement account setup with Wind Industrial to enable faster ordering and procurement of materials (08:48)
Initiate creation of a racking schedule/tracker file for McLean and new projects to document weekly progress and maintain organization (28:45)
Order six-inch pipe couplings, cones, and mechanical tees and arrange for next-day pickup from One Industrial due to urgent timing needs (05:22)
Monitor GPC site for any air leaks during branch line installation and report immediately to coordinate repairs if leaks are detected (06:36)
criticalRisks: Air pressure testing is ongoing at Uniqlo, with equipment delivery anticipated, while the GPC site is addressing pipe leaks and expediting procurement for critical parts.
System A partially completed with gap due to flu pipe delay.
## Notes ## **Project Progress and Site Updates** The teams are steadily advancing on pipe rack installations and system air testing despite some delays caused by missing components.

provider_decision.reason: Streaming model tools enabled on the configured AI SDK provider. Vercel AI Gateway is preferred when AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is configured; direct OpenAI requires AI_PROVIDER_PATH=openai or no gateway key. Disable by setting AI_ASSISTANT_DISABLE_STREAMING_MODEL_TOOLS=true.
